title: Level 5.1

{{Quote|Maybe there is some comfort to be found between these dreaded beige walls after all...}}
---- ---- {{Class 5|hexagon=1|class=1e - Environmental|color-h=28|color-s=68%|color-l=68%|safety=Docile Entities|entity= '''Level 5.1''' is a sublevel of [[Level 5]] typically accessed by random noclipping in its parent level. It was discovered on February 13th, 1981, having since garnered the presence of many colonies due to its habitability.
---- ==Appearance==
{{Blurimage|file=hotelthing1.jpg|width=300px|caption=A basic hallway in Level 5.1.}} {{Blurimage|file=hotelthing3.jpg|width=300px|caption=Simple, disorganized furniture.}} {{Blurimage|file=Bedpoolexpanded.jpg|width=300px|caption=Hey, why not take a dip?}} {{Blurimage|file=Sundownn.jpg|width=300px|caption=A hallway during the Sundown Event.}} In its most simple areas, '''Level 5.1''' presents a balmy complex of vintage hotel hallways bathed in a dingy tan hue. Articles of unmovable furniture such as chairs and tables are irregularly scattered in nonsensical places, constructed of plastic albeit painted to mimic other materials. Abstract paintings depicting mundane objects are hung along the walls, along with clocks and wall-mounted thermometers. The carpeted floors are rough with the dried stains of various beverages, although retain vibrant shades of turquoise and violet. The halls of '''Level 5.1''' are rigid and irregular; longer stretches will typically drift slightly yet sharply at odd angles, while true turns may be unnecessarily complex in layout. A faint scent of chlorine lingers in the decently humid air despite there being no traces of such chemical. The odor is apparent regardless of one's location in '''Level 5.1.''' '''Level 5.1''' maintains a constant temperature of 33°C (98.6°F) throughout the edifice. The lights of the level, despite being significantly searing by themselves, do not seem to exactly cause the heat coursing throughout '''Level 5.1.''' Speculation has lead to theories stating that heat dissipates far less efficiently in the level; these have yielded no results in being disproven and is thus believed to be the cause of the high temperatures. The level's lights additionally emit UV rays, mimicking the sun's effects on Earth. While a temperature of 33°C would typically be uncomfortable for most wanderers, an anomalous effect causes the temperature to be nearly unnoticeable to all individuals. Sunburns are the most notable threat in the level; fortunately, sunblock is a frequent commodity that can commonly be found in tubes. Sunburns acquired in this level do, however, heal unusually rapidly once given relief. One may also identify the temperature with the wall-mounted thermometers found in '''Level 5.1.''' '''Level 5.1''' seldom features large, potted false plants scattered rarely across the level. While being made of majorly polyester, they exhibit many properties similar to real plants, absorbing water and emitting oxygen. These plants are the primary source of '''Insulation Pollen,''' a level-exclusive object that provides exceptional heatproofing capabilities. '''Insulation Pollen''' is a pink, chunky powder produced by the flowers of '''Level 5.1's''' false plants, supposedly providing the plants themselves resistance to the level's heat. Mixing '''Insulation Pollen''' with [[Object 1|Almond Water]] results in '''Insulation Gel,''' which can be applied to one's skin to provide complete protection against temperatures up to 55°C (131°F) for up to three hours. '''Insulation Gel''' may also be found in bottles across the level, branded with a label reading, "[[Tezo's Art Supplies]]". Marked hotel rooms are an uncommon feature of the level, although tend to appear in large clusters. Framed by the vibrant wood lining the bottoms of the walls and numbered arbitrarily, newly-discovered doors are locked and can only be opened by the respective key. Keys are typically found lodged into a wall near their respective door which may simply be removed with a light tug. If a key is considered "lost" by conventional standards, it will appear in the given dent while not being observed for extended periods of time. The interior of a hotel room is a circular platform stationed in the center of a small pool, fit with much of the furniture and commodities present in a standard room. A bridge leads from the entrance to this platform, lacking sideguards that could prevent one from falling into the shallow pool. A television, a twin bed, and a nightstand are the only regular items known to appear in all rooms. Additionally, the nightstand will always feature a Bible with its cover scribbled out in red marker and a ram's skull drawn on the back. The television, regardless of whether it is powered, constantly displays black-and-white weather reports from the 1950's. In rooms featuring a remote control device, attempting to access other channels will display a pink checker pattern, with various Bible verses being rapidly displayed in a glowing red font. The speakers blast loud static noise, which temporarily damages the speakers and results in muffled audio for ~5-6 days. Why rooms consistently portray a theme of Christian sin is unknown, though it is believed to have ties to the rarely-occurring Sundown Event. The Sundown Event
The Sundown Event is a phenomenon exclusive to '''Level 5.1,''' characterized by its connection to the treachery layer of Dante's Inferno. They are an extremely uncommon occurrence, with only 22 instances recorded since the level's official discovery. Sundown Events are known to only occur during the summer months, however - that being June, July, and August. Prior to the occurrence of a Sundown Event, '''Level 5.1''' will alter itself to fit the "conditions" required for the event to begin. Paintings will begin merging into the walls, gradually transforming into windows that reveal [[The Grand Medium|a staticky void]] beyond the structure. Existing fluorescent lights in the level will begin to fade or flicker, and all light sources brought into the level will instantly burn out. All doors existing within the level shut themselves, remaining locked until the end of the Sundown Event. This state lasts ~8 hours, eventually transitioning into the Sundown Event itself. The Sundown Event is marked by the burnout of all lights in the level and the replacement of the aforementioned static void with a hazy, warm light. The level's temperature will gradually decrease to ~12°C (53.6°F), tending to cause thermal shock within any long-term residents of '''Level 5.1.''' Objects native to the level, whether simply items or furniture, will merge into the walls and floors with the exception of televisions. All televisions during the Sundown Event display flashing text that states "TREACHERY" in bolded black lettering in front of a white background. Additionally, the water in hotel rooms will freeze over, with all individuals in these rooms sensing a numb cold from the waist down. These events interestingly show many connections to the ninth layer of Dante's Inferno. The Sundown Event ends after approximately three days. All modifications to '''Level 5.1's''' environment will revert, including the disappearance of windows and reappearance of furniture. When all modifications have successfully reverted, the lights will flicker on, concluding the Sundown Event.
==Entities==
Level 5.1 is home to a variety of docile entities—the most common of which are [[Entity 28|Trotting Snails]], [[Entity 2|Innocuous Windows]], and [[Entity 69|Lightlurkers]]. In very few pools, one may find a more harmless strain of [[Hydrolitis Plague]], merely having mild flu-like symptoms.
==Colonies and Outposts==
===A.E.G. Waypoint Scorch=== *Colony founded by the A.E.G.; *Consists of 12 officials and 0 members; *Primarily serves as habitation for officials; *Does not assist wanderers or accept members.
===Sevitestus=== *Town founded by the [[Republic of Aurielle]]; *Consists of ~40 officials and ~550 members; *Habitation-focused, although is known to harvest materials from the level for economic purposes; *Accepts members.
===Sundown Orange=== *Base founded by [[Tezo's Art Supplies]]; *Consists of 34 total artists; *Serves as a location for art creation, as well as collecting '''Insulation Pollen''' to develop art supplies; *May accept certain members.
Other settlements are known to reside in '''Level 5.1;''' though most are generally insignificant. ==Addendum== (Editor's Note: 'Certain sections of the following entry may be inconsistent due to errors in translation from Vietnamese. Additionally, some sections of the page have been whitened out by the heat in Level 5.1, with the best of efforts being made to fill in the indecipherable spaces. Potential errors have been highlighted in square brackets.')
• Recovered Journal Entry •
"To whoever finds this journal, I have likely either returned to reality or been consumed by the horrors lurking in these depths. I have been told many times that seeking an exit from the Wake will only drag you into a fruitless endeavor of persistent false hope, though I'm certain any [higher deity could not be this cruel.] I am optimistic to survive. From the last [[[Level 5|nightmarish hotel]]] I attempted to find residence in, I have found this location to be far more forgiving. I no longer feel the lingering doom breathing on my neck or the [sense of anxiety following me by every step] I make. Yes, the location courses with an immense heat, but in contrast to the other threats abound, it is not much of a burden. '''The Summer Resort''' does well to accommodate that. In my torpid amblings, I stumbled across a section of hotel rooms, numbered in symbols I did not recognize. Perhaps they were not meant for my comprehension; I know now that [seeking answers will only result in further confusion] in this unstable dimension. Without any distraction, I used a nearby key to unlock it, where I was met with a small platform for a living space situated in a shallow pool. This is where I will rest tonight. I noticed sunburn spots [accumulating on my skin] in the moments before finding a room. Fortunately, sunblock is abundant here, which I consider generous for this dimension. I am reminded of my days on vacation as a child; while monotonous tan-hued hallways were frequent where I stayed, there was always excitement for the next day to overshadow the unsettling feeling. Now, I am granted this respite in the form of [basic survival] as opposed to true joy - still, I am thankful. Maybe there is some comfort to be found between these dreaded beige walls after all."
==Entrances and Exits==
Entrances *Random noclipping in [[Level 5]] may lead to this level. *Entering a bunker door in [[Level 4]] will lead to this level. *Noclipping into an [[Entity 85|Aggressive Light]] in [[Level 9.7]] is rumored to send one to '''Level 5.1.''' *Having a karma rating of 21-49 on [[Level 995]] has a chance to send you here. *Hotel rooms on the middle floors of [[Level 11.3]]'s skyscrapers will take you to this level. Exits *Sliding doors in areas resembling lobbies are an uncommon yet feasible sight; entering them may lead to [[:Category:Aquatic|any level with a significant water presence]]. *Entering a trapdoor in a marked room will lead to [[Level 2]] if rushing water is heard on the other side; this will lead to [[Level 4]] if rain is heard, or [[Level 7]] if crashing waves are heard. *[[Object 38|Doom Locks]] can be found on doors in this level, which will lead one to any safe level once completed. *Sleeping in a room with a corrupted television sends one to [[Level 0.8]].
